title: Top 5 Free Kanban Boards Compared
excerpt: KanbanFlow, WeKan, MeisterTask, Restyaboard, and KanbanAI — compared on signup, open source, and built-in AI for planning and tasks.
author: Kanban AI Team
body:
# Top 5 Free Kanban Boards Compared

Here are five free kanban tools worth comparing. The table leans on **AI**: whether the product helps you plan from a rough idea, generate and refine work items, and answer in context on the cards themselves. **KanbanAI** is the one we ship ([MIT on GitHub](https://github.com/orholam/kanban_ai), guest board at [/kanban](https://kanbanai.dev/kanban) with no account).

## 1. KanbanFlow

**Pros:**
- No account required for basic boards
- Clean, simple interface
- Timer and Pomodoro technique support
- WIP (Work In Progress) limits
- Free forever for personal use

**Cons:**
- Limited features without account
- No team collaboration without signup
- Basic customization options

**Best for:** Personal task management and quick planning sessions.

## 2. WeKan

**Pros:**
- Completely open source
- Self-hostable option available
- No registration needed for public boards
- Card comments and attachments
- Multiple board views

**Cons:**
- Interface can feel dated
- Limited mobile experience
- Self-hosting requires technical knowledge

**Best for:** Teams comfortable with self-hosting or open-source solutions.

## 3. MeisterTask

**Pros:**
- Beautiful, modern interface
- Free tier with no time limit
- Can create boards without immediate signup
- Good mobile apps
- Integrations available

**Cons:**
- Eventually requires account for full features
- Limited boards on free plan
- Some features locked behind paywall

**Best for:** Teams that want a polished experience and don't mind eventually signing up.

## 4. Restyaboard

**Pros:**
- Open source and self-hostable
- No signup for public boards
- Card templates available
- Activity tracking
- Free community edition

**Cons:**
- Setup can be complex
- Smaller community than commercial tools
- Limited documentation

**Best for:** Organizations wanting full control over their data.

## 5. KanbanAI

**Pros:**
- **Open source (MIT)** — inspect and fork on [GitHub](https://github.com/orholam/kanban_ai)
- **Guest kanban** — [/kanban](https://kanbanai.dev/kanban) without signing up; sign in for cloud sync and collaboration
- **AI planning** — describe a project and get structured phases, tasks, and a living roadmap you can adjust
- **AI on the cards** — @mention the assistant in task threads for concise, in-context help; iterate breakdowns and priorities as you drag work across columns
- Responsive UI on desktop and phone browsers

**Cons:**
- Hosted SaaS (not a self-host product you run yourself)
- Smaller third-party integration catalog than the biggest PM suites

**Best for:** Teams who want AI baked into planning and day-to-day task work, not bolted on elsewhere.

## Quick Comparison

| Tool | No signup | Open source | AI planning & roadmaps | AI assistant on tasks |
|------|-----------|-------------|-------------------------|----------------------|
| KanbanFlow | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| WeKan | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| MeisterTask | ⚠️ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Restyaboard | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| KanbanAI | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

*AI planning & roadmaps* = turning a goal or idea into phased work inside the product. *AI assistant on tasks* = model replies in the task/card thread (not a separate chat app). Third-party Power-Ups or Zapier hooks do not count here.

## Verdict

**KanbanFlow** is still the fastest anonymous dabble. **WeKan** and **Restyaboard** win for classic OSS and self-hosting without AI. **MeisterTask** is the polished commercial pick.

**KanbanAI** is the only option above with native AI for both **planning** and **per-task help** — plus guest access and an open codebase. Try it at [kanbanai.dev](https://kanbanai.dev).

**Remember:** “Free” always has limits — compare guest vs signed-in features before you commit a team.
